creator: michaelo@FreeBSD.org
text:
Looking at:
> $ openssl s_client -connect smtp.freebsd.org:587 -starttls smtp
> CONNECTED(00000003)
> depth=2 C = US, O = Internet Security Research Group, CN = ISRG Root X1
> verify return:1
> depth=1 C = US, O = Let's Encrypt, CN = R3
> verify return:1
> depth=0 CN = smtp.freebsd.org
> verify return:1

> Certificate chain
>  0 s:CN = smtp.freebsd.org

The server sends a huge list of DNs for accepted client certificate issuers. First of all, I highly doubt that this server accepts client certs at all, but that really looks like the output of "certctl list".

While I am not an expert in Postfix config, I believe that "tls_append_default_CA = yes" or "smtpd_tls_CApath = /etc/ssl/certs" is needless ly set. In mod_ssl it would be SSLCADNRequestFile/SSLCADNRequestPath.

The output does not cause trouble, but:
* The field is limited to 65 535 bytes and when it exceeds OpenSSL on the server side will send an alert. See https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc5246.html#section-7.4.4,  DistinguishedName certificate_authorities<0..2^16-1>;
* useless traffic is produced between client and server

Postfix config for smtp.FreeBSD.org should be revised.
